info.title: RAIL0 Payment API
info.description: REST API for initiating and authorizing stablecoin payments via the RAIL0 smart contract (EIP-3009 / EIP-712).

post.description: Called by the payer. Creates a payment record and returns the EIP-712 typed-data payload for the payer to sign immediately. The `signingPayload` contains `domain`, `types`, and `message` in standard EIP-712 format: wallet users pass it verbatim to `eth_signTypedData_v4`; backends or SDKs with direct key access compute the same hash with any EIP-712 library and sign it with secp256k1 — the result is identical. The `mode` field determines the flow: `authorize` places funds in escrow for later capture; `charge` is a one-shot authorize+capture with no escrow window. The nonce in the `signingPayload` is derived using the corresponding prefix (`RAIL0.AUTHORIZE` or `RAIL0.CHARGE`), so a signature for one mode cannot be reused for the other. The signed components (v, r, s) must then be submitted to `PUT /payments/{paymentId}/signature`, after which the payee triggers on-chain submission via `POST /payments/{paymentId}/authorize` or `POST /payments/{paymentId}/charge`.

201.description: Payment intent created. Sign the returned `signingPayload` and submit to `PUT /payments/{paymentId}/signature`.

422.description: Validation error (invalid addresses, expired deadlines, fee config, etc.).

put.description: Called by the payer after signing the `signingPayload` returned by `POST /payments`. Stores the EIP-3009 signature (v, r, s) server-side so that the payee can later trigger on-chain submission via `POST /payments/{paymentId}/authorize` without needing any input from the payer. Idempotent: re-submitting the same signature for the same payment is accepted.

200.description: Signature stored. The payee may now call `POST /payments/{paymentId}/authorize`.

409.description: Payment already authorized on-chain; signature can no longer be updated.

422.description: Signature does not recover to the expected payer address.

post.description: Called by the payee to build the unsigned `capture()` transaction. The API validates the amount against the current on-chain state and returns a ready-to-sign EIP-1559 transaction. The payee signs it with their wallet (`eth_signTransaction`) and submits the result to `POST /payments/{paymentId}/capture/submit`.

422.description: Amount is zero, exceeds capturable balance, or authorizationExpiry has already passed.

post.description: Submits a signed capture transaction to the network. The payee is the on-chain sender and pays gas. The `POST /payments/{paymentId}/capture` prepare step is optional: clients that can construct and sign the transaction independently (e.g. via an SDK with ABI knowledge) may call this endpoint directly with a self-built signed transaction. The `paymentId` in the path must match the one encoded in the transaction calldata and must already exist in the API (created via `POST /payments`) so that the stored payment config can be used to decode and return the updated state.

422.description: Invalid or malformed signed transaction, or on-chain revert (NotPayee, AuthorizationExpired, InvalidCaptureAmount).

post.summary: Release escrowed funds back to the payer
post.description: Submits the RAIL0 `release()` transaction on-chain. Returns all remaining escrowed funds to the payer after `authorizationExpiry` has passed and no full capture occurred. Unlike capture, void, and refund, `release` has no `msg.sender` restriction — anyone can trigger it, so the API acts as relayer and pays gas. No request body or signing step required.

422.description: On-chain revert — authorizationExpiry not yet reached, or nothing left to release (capturableAmount is 0).

post.description: Called by the payee to relay the payer's previously stored EIP-3009 signature to the RAIL0 `authorize()` function. Funds are pulled from the payer into escrow. The payee is the on-chain transaction sender and pays gas. No request body is required: the signature was already deposited by the payer via `PUT /payments/{paymentId}/signature`.

409.description: Payment already authorized (on-chain state already exists).

422.description: Payer signature not yet submitted, or on-chain revert (e.g. AuthorizationExpired, TokenNotAccepted).

post.description: Called by the payee to relay the payer's EIP-3009 signature (mode=charge) to the RAIL0 `charge()` function. Funds are pulled from the payer and immediately distributed to payee and feeReceiver — no escrow window. The payee is the on-chain transaction sender and pays gas. The signature must have been deposited via `PUT /payments/{paymentId}/signature` with a payment created with `mode=charge`.

422.description: Payer signature not yet submitted, payment was created with mode=authorize, or on-chain revert (e.g. AuthorizationExpired, TokenNotAccepted).

post.description: Builds an unsigned ERC-20 `approve(spender, amount)` transaction where `spender` is the RAIL0 contract and the token is taken from the payment config. Required before the payee can broadcast a refund. The payee signs the returned transaction and submits it to `POST /payments/{paymentId}/approve/submit`.

200.description: Unsigned approve transaction ready to sign.

post.summary: Broadcast a signed token approval transaction
post.description: Submits a signed ERC-20 `approve` transaction to the network. Once confirmed, the RAIL0 contract is authorized to pull up to `amount` tokens from the payee's wallet, enabling refund operations. The `POST /payments/{paymentId}/approve` prepare step is optional: clients that can construct and sign the transaction independently may call this endpoint directly.

Called by the payee to cancel the authorization and return all escrowed funds to the payer. Void is only possible while `capturableAmount > 0`. The API returns an unsigned transaction; the payee signs it and submits to `POST /payments/{paymentId}/void/submit`.

200.description: Unsigned void transaction ready to sign.

post.description: Submits a signed void transaction to the network. All remaining escrowed funds are returned to the payer. The `POST /payments/{paymentId}/void` prepare step is optional: clients that can construct and sign the transaction independently may call this endpoint directly.

422.description: Invalid or malformed signed transaction, or on-chain revert (NotPayee, NothingToVoid).

post.description: Called by the payee to refund a previously captured amount to the payer. The payee must have approved the RAIL0 contract as an ERC-20 spender on the token for at least `amount` before submitting. The API returns an unsigned transaction; the payee signs it and submits to `POST /payments/{paymentId}/refund/submit`.

200.description: Unsigned refund transaction ready to sign.

422.description: Amount is zero, exceeds refundable balance, or refundExpiry has already passed.

post.description: Submits a signed refund transaction to the network. The specified amount is pulled from the payee's wallet back to the payer via `transferFrom`. The `POST /payments/{paymentId}/refund` prepare step is optional: clients that can construct and sign the transaction independently may call this endpoint directly.

422.description: Invalid or malformed signed transaction, or on-chain revert (NotPayee, RefundExpired, InvalidRefundAmount, TransferFailed).

Address.description: EVM address (checksummed or lowercase hex, 20 bytes).

Uint256String.description: Non-negative integer encoded as a decimal string to avoid JSON numeric precision loss.

PaymentConfig.description: Immutable payment configuration that maps 1-to-1 to the RAIL0 `Payment` Solidity struct.

payer.description: Buyer address. Funds are pulled from this address.

payee.description: Merchant address. Authorized to capture, void, and refund.

token.description: EIP-3009-capable ERC-20 token address (must be accepted by the RAIL0 deployment).

maxAmount.description: Upper bound on the authorized amount (fits in uint120).

authorizationExpiry.description: Unix timestamp (seconds). Capture must happen before this; release opens after.

refundExpiry.description: Unix timestamp (seconds). Refund must happen before this. Must be >= authorizationExpiry.

feeBps.description: Fee in basis points (0 = no fee, 10000 = 100%).

feeReceiver.description: Recipient of the fee on each capture. Use the zero address (0x0000…0000) when feeBps is 0.

CreatePaymentRequest.description: Parameters needed to create a payment intent. The API generates a unique `paymentId` and constructs the EIP-712 signing payload.

amount.description: Exact amount to authorize (in token base units). Must be > 0 and <= payment.maxAmount.

chainId.description: EVM chain ID of the target network.

mode.description: `authorize` — funds are held in escrow and captured later by the payee. `charge` — one-shot: funds are immediately distributed to payee and feeReceiver with no escrow window. The two modes use different EIP-3009 nonce prefixes; a signature produced for one cannot be submitted for the other.

EIP712Domain.description: EIP-712 domain for the token contract (used by EIP-3009 TransferWithAuthorization).

EIP3009Message.description: Message fields for the EIP-3009 TransferWithAuthorization typed-data signature.

value.description: Amount to transfer (same as the requested authorization amount).

nonce.description: keccak256(NONCE_PREFIX, paymentId, configHash) where NONCE_PREFIX is `RAIL0.AUTHORIZE` for mode=authorize and `RAIL0.CHARGE` for mode=charge. Binds the signature to the exact Payment configuration and operation type.

SigningPayload.description: EIP-712 typed-data structure that the payer must sign. The `domain`, `types`, and `message` fields follow the EIP-712 standard. Signing options: (a) wallet users pass this object verbatim to `eth_signTypedData_v4`; (b) backends with direct key access compute `keccak256('\x19\x01' || domainSeparator || hashStruct(message))` with any EIP-712 library and sign with secp256k1. Both approaches produce the same (v, r, s) components to submit to `PUT /payments/{paymentId}/signature`.

paymentId.description: Unique identifier for this payment (generated by the API, bytes32 hex).

configHash.description: EIP-712 hash of the Payment struct over the RAIL0 domain. Commits the signature to the exact payment terms.

rail0Contract.description: Address of the RAIL0 contract on the target chain.

PrepareTransactionResponse.description: An unsigned EIP-1559 transaction ready for the payee to sign. Signing options: (a) wallet users pass `unsignedTransaction` to `eth_signTransaction`; (b) backends with direct key access sign the RLP blob with any secp256k1 library (e.g. `wallet.signTransaction` in ethers.js). Submit the resulting signed RLP to the corresponding `/submit` endpoint.

unsignedTransaction.description: RLP-encoded unsigned EIP-1559 transaction (type 2). Pass to `eth_signTransaction` (browser wallet) or sign directly with a secp256k1 library if the key is available in-process.

to.description: RAIL0 contract address (informational; already encoded in `unsignedTransaction`).

nonce.description: Transaction nonce for the payee's account.

maxPriorityFeePerGas.description: EIP-1559 max priority fee per gas (wei).

gasLimit.description: Estimated gas limit with a safety margin.

signedTransaction.description: RLP-encoded signed EIP-1559 transaction as returned by `eth_signTransaction`.

CapturePaymentRequest.description: Amount to capture from escrow. May be less than the total capturable balance for a partial capture.

amount.description: Amount to capture (in token base units). Must be > 0 and <= current capturableAmount.

transactionHash.description: On-chain transaction hash of the `capture()` call.

capturedAmount.description: Gross amount captured in this call (before fee deduction). Matches the `amount` parameter passed to the contract.

feeAmount.description: Fee deducted from this capture and sent to feeReceiver. '0' when feeBps is 0.

capturableAmount.description: Remaining escrowed amount still available for future captures.

refundableAmount.description: Cumulative amount already captured and eligible for refund (until refundExpiry).

authorizationExpiry.description: Unix timestamp after which further captures are no longer possible.

ApproveRequest.description: Amount to approve on the token contract. Setting this to the maximum expected refund (or `type(uint256).max` for unlimited) avoids repeated approvals.

amount.description: Allowance to grant the RAIL0 contract (in token base units). Use '115792089237316195423570985008687907853269984665640564039457584007913129639935' for unlimited approval.

transactionHash.description: On-chain transaction hash of the `approve()` call.

token.description: Token contract on which the approval was set.

spender.description: RAIL0 contract address that was approved as spender.

RefundPaymentRequest.description: Amount to refund to the payer. Must be > 0 and <= current refundableAmount.

amount.description: Amount to refund (in token base units). Must be > 0 and <= current refundableAmount.

transactionHash.description: On-chain transaction hash of the `release()` call.

releasedAmount.description: Amount returned to the payer (was the full remaining capturableAmount).

transactionHash.description: On-chain transaction hash of the `charge()` call.

chargedAmount.description: Gross amount charged (before fee deduction). Matches the `amount` parameter passed to the contract.

feeAmount.description: Fee sent to feeReceiver. '0' when feeBps is 0.

refundableAmount.description: Amount eligible for refund (equals chargedAmount; the full gross amount is tracked as refundable by the contract).

transactionHash.description: On-chain transaction hash of the `void()` call.

releasedAmount.description: Amount returned to the payer (was the full capturableAmount).

transactionHash.description: On-chain transaction hash of the `refund()` call.

refundedAmount.description: Amount refunded to the payer in this call.

refundableAmount.description: Remaining refundable balance after this refund.

PayerSignatureRequest.description: EIP-712 signature over the `signingPayload` returned by `POST /payments`. The payer may produce this with `eth_signTypedData_v4` (browser wallet) or with any EIP-712 library that has direct access to the private key — the on-chain verification only checks the recovered address.

status.description: Confirms the signature was accepted and stored.

recoveredPayer.description: The address recovered from the signature — should match `payment.payer`. Included for client-side verification.

transactionHash.description: On-chain transaction hash of the `authorize()` call.

capturableAmount.description: Amount now held in escrow and available for capture (equals the authorized amount).

authorizationExpiry.description: Unix timestamp after which capture is no longer possible and release becomes available.

message.example: The authorization window has already passed.
